content.problemStatement:
You're given an array AAA of length NNN, and an integer KKK.

AAA is said to be a nice array if there exists an array BBB of length NNN satisfying the following conditions:


For every index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), Bi=‚ĆäAiK‚ĆčB_i = \left\lfloor \frac{A_i}{K} \right\rfloorBi‚Äč=‚ĆäKAi‚Äč‚Äč‚Ćč, or Bi=‚ĆąAiK‚ĆČB_i = \left\lceil \frac{A_i}{K} \right\rceilBi‚Äč=‚ĆąKAi‚Äč‚Äč‚ĆČ; and
B1+B2+‚Ä¶+BN=0B_1 + B_2 + \ldots + B_N = 0B1‚Äč+B2‚Äč+‚Ä¶+BN‚Äč=0.
Is AAA a nice array?

Note that ‚Ćä  ‚Ćč\left\lfloor \ \ \right\rfloor‚Ćä  ‚Ćč and ‚Ćą  ‚ĆČ\left\lceil \ \ \right\rceil‚Ćą  ‚ĆČ denote the floor and ceiling functions, respectively.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK ‚ÄĒ the length of array AAA and the division parameter.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single string denoting the answer: `"YES"` if AAA is a nice array, and `"NO"` otherwise (without quotes).


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e., the strings `NO`, `No`, `nO`, and `no` will all be treated as equivalent.
constraints: It's guaranteed that the sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Choose B=[1,2,‚ąí1,2]B = [1, 2, -1, 2]B=[1,2,‚ąí1,2], which satisfies Bi=‚ĆäAiK‚ĆčB_i = \left\lfloor \frac{A_i}{K} \right\rfloorBi‚Äč=‚ĆäKAi‚Äč‚Äč‚Ćč for all iii. The sum of this array is 000, so AAA is nice.
Test case 333: No valid BBB exists, so AAA isn't nice.
Test case 444: B=[‚ąí1,1]B = [-1, 1]B=[‚ąí1,1] is a valid array, so AAA is nice.
